blunt_answer: CapCard can be great if it stays an internal evidence marketplace first; it is not ready as a public marketplace.
top_strengths: first working EML Puzzle Evidence Kernel card
exact blockers for Qwen rather than hand-waving
top_weaknesses: marketplace UX is still mock data
human approval workflow lacks signed reviewer fields
first_working_card_status: EML Puzzle Evidence Kernel works as an internal draft and strong candidate.
second_card_status: Qwen Puzzle Curriculum Pack remains blocked by warn/unknown rows.
product_moat: The moat is disciplined evidence-boundary workflow: validators, traceability, human review, and no-go claim enforcement in one card lifecycle.
next_7_day_plan: repair Qwen rows or retire the candidate
next_30_day_plan: pilot with three to five internal cards
add review ownership and audit log
draft public-release policy but keep public blocked
